items.mistake: Dividing without guarding zero or missing denominators
items.fix: Use clip(lower=ε), replace(0, np.nan), or np.where before ratios; document null propagation for serving.
items.why: Inf and NaN columns break training and silently poison inference when production rows have zero sqft or missing counts.
items.example: df['price_per_sqft'] = df['price'] / df['sqft'] yields inf for sqft=0 rows—clip sqft to at least 1 or drop invalid rows first.

items.mistake: Computing customer aggregates using future transactions
items.fix: Filter orders to timestamps strictly before each row's prediction cutoff; use point-in-time joins or asof merges.
items.why: Including post-label activity leaks the future into features and makes offline churn AUC unrealistically high.
items.example: For churn at 2024-01-01, order_count must count only orders with order_date < 2024-01-01, not the full customer lifetime.

items.mistake: Blind all-pairs interaction products on dozens of numeric columns
items.fix: Add interactions justified by domain logic or use regularized models with deliberate cross-terms; prune collinear pairs.
items.why: n numeric columns yield n(n-1)/2 products—sparse data cannot support hundreds of correlated interaction terms.
items.example: Multiplying every census column on 40 raw fields creates 780 interaction columns with 500 training rows—severe overfitting.

items.mistake: Using raw hour 0–23 without cyclical encoding for distance-sensitive models
items.fix: For linear or distance-based models, add sin/cos hour pairs or treat hour as categorical after encoding.
items.why: Euclidean distance treats hour 23 as far from hour 0 even though they are adjacent in time.
items.example: KNN on [hour=23, hour=0] sees distance 23; sin/cos pair maps both near the same point on the unit circle.
